# babel-plugin-nordic-module-resolver

A [Babel](http://babeljs.io) plugin to add a new resolver for your modules when compiling your code using Babel.

## Description

This plugin based on [babel-plugin-module-resolver](https://github.com/tleunen/babel-plugin-module-resolver) allows you to:

- Add new "root" directories that contain your modules.
- Setup a custom alias for directories, specific files, or even other npm modules, simplifying the require/import paths in your project.

  It will allow you to work faster since you won't need to calculate how many levels of directory you have to go up before accessing the file.

  For example, instead of using complex relative paths like `../../../../utils/my-utils`, you can write `utils/my-utils`.

```js
// Use this:
import MyUtilFn from 'utils/MyUtilFn';
// Instead of that:
import MyUtilFn from '../../../../utils/MyUtilFn';

// And it also work with require calls
// Use this:
const MyUtilFn = require('utils/MyUtilFn');
// Instead of that:
const MyUtilFn = require('../../../../utils/MyUtilFn');
```

## Installation

```sh
npm install --save-dev babel-plugin-nordic-module-resolver
```

## Usage

### Via `.babelrc`

```json
{
  "plugins": [
    [
      "babel-plugin-nordic-module-resolver",
      {
        "root": ["./src"],
        "alias": {
          "test": "./test",
          "underscore": "lodash"
        }
      }
    ]
  ]
}
```

## Documentation

`babel-plugin-nordic-module-resolver` is already configured on Nordic babel presets see [how to use it](https://nordic.adminml.com/docs/module-aliases).

See the [original module docs](https://github.com/tleunen/babel-plugin-module-resolver/blob/master/DOCS.md) to see all options.
